This LTL property ensures that if the sensor limits are exceeded, the autopilot will eventually transition to the pullup state and stay there as long as the pilot is not in control (standby) and the system is supported without failures (apfail).  The LTL property is a conjunction of three conditions. The first condition, G(!(standby) & !apfail & limits -> F pullup), ensures that if the pilot is not in control, the system is supported, and the sensor limits are exceeded, then the autopilot will eventually transition to the pullup state. The second condition, G(!(standby) & !apfail & limits -> G(pullup -> G pullup)), ensures that the autopilot will remain in the pullup state as long as the pilot is not in control and the system is supported. The third condition, G(!(standby) & !apfail & limits -> G(!(standby) & !apfail & limits -> pullup)) ensures that the autopilot will only transition to the pullup state when the pilot is not in control and the system is supported, and the sensor limits are exceeded. This ensures that the autopilot will not transition to the pullup state if the sensor limits are not exceeded. The previously given LTL property might not capture the input_natural_language because it does not ensure that the autopilot will stay in the pullup state once it transitions to that state.
